title: OneLogin Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**OneLogin** is a cloud SSO / IAM platform (One Identity) exposing a REST API suite under `https://<subdomain>.onelogin.com`. This connector — `OneLoginConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "onelogin"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_client_credentials"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a OneLogin tenant:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Auth | `/auth/oauth2/v2/token` | OAuth2 client_credentials → Bearer access_token (cached) |
| Users | `/api/2/users` | List, get, create, update, delete users; per-user role/app assignments |
| Apps | `/api/2/apps` | List + read SAML/OIDC apps; assign apps to users |
| Roles | `/api/2/roles` | List + read RBAC roles |
| Groups | `/api/2/groups` | List directory groups |
| Privileges | `/api/2/privileges` | List custom privileges |
| Mappings | `/api/2/mappings` | List user-to-app mapping rules |
| Events | `/api/2/events` | Audit log (filterable by since / event_type_id) |

The connector normalises users + events into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and silently refreshes the cached Bearer token on the first 401. Strict SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only — all HTTP is delegated to `client/http_client.py::OneLoginHTTPClient`.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate OneLogin API credentials

1. Sign in to your OneLogin admin console.
2. Open **Developers → API Credentials**.
3. Click **New Credential** and choose the smallest scope that satisfies your use case (`Manage all`, `Read all`, `Manage users`, etc.).
4. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** — the secret is shown only once.
5. Note your **subdomain** (the prefix in `acme.onelogin.com`).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → OneLogin**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `subdomain` (text) | e.g. `acme` |
| `client_id` (text) | API credential client_id |
| `client_secret` (secret) | API credential client_secret |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override the derived `https://{subdomain}.onelogin.com` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the three required fields are non-empty (it does NOT call the API), persists the config, initialises the `OneLoginHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)`. The first authenticated call (or `authenticate()` explicitly) exchanges client_credentials for a Bearer token.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists 1 user as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On persistent 401 (after silent refresh) → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with a structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
OneLogin uses **OAuth2 client_credentials** (`AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_client_credentials"`).

### Credential model

- `client_id` + `client_secret` are sent via HTTP Basic auth (`Authorization: Basic base64(client_id:client_secret)`) on the token endpoint.
- The body is `grant_type=client_credentials` (form-encoded).
- The response carries an `access_token` + `expires_in` (typically 36000 s / 10 h). The HTTP client caches it until `expires_at - 60s`.
- API calls send the access token as `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

### Token endpoint

```
POST https://{subdomain}.onelogin.com/auth/oauth2/v2/token
Authorization: Basic base64(client_id:client_secret)
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=client_credentials
```

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `subdomain` + `client_id` + `client_secret`. Initialises `OneLoginHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any OneLogin endpoint. |
| `authorize(auth_code, state)` | Surface-compat with BaseConnector — delegates to `authenticate()`. |
| `authenticate()` | Performs the client_credentials POST, caches the access token, returns `TokenInfo`. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /api/2/users?limit=1` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | `_ensure_authenticated()` refreshes the cached token if stale. |
| 401 mid-request | HTTP client clears the cache, re-authenticates, replays the original request **once**. |

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `subdomain` | text | yes | `self.subdomain` | Used by `compute_base_url(subdomain)` to build `https://{subdomain}.onelogin.com` |
| `client_id` | text | yes | `self.client_id` | Basic-auth username on the token endpoint |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | Basic-auth password on the token endpoint |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` | Override for staging / region. Defaults to derived value |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["subdomain", "client_id", "client_secret"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_TOKEN_PATH = "/auth/oauth2/v2/token"
_API_PREFIX = "/api/2"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `OneLoginConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::OneLoginHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `subdomain` + `client_id` + `client_secret`; persist config; initialise `OneLoginHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
Delegates to `authenticate()` — OneLogin client_credentials has no auth-code exchange.

### `async authenticate() -> TokenInfo`
POST `/auth/oauth2/v2/token` with HTTP Basic + `grant_type=client_credentials`. Caches the access token (60s skew before expiry). Returns `TokenInfo`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 user via `GET /api/2/users?limit=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Cursor-paginated iteration over `/api/2/users` → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Persists `after_cursor` via `set_metadata` so incremental syncs resume.

children.title: Users
children.content:
### `async list_users(*, limit=50, after_cursor=None, email=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/users` with cursor pagination + optional email filter.

### `async get_user(user_id) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/users/{user_id}`.

### `async create_user(email, firstname, lastname, *, username=None, password=None, role_ids=None) -> Dict`
`POST /api/2/users` — provisions a new user. Password (when provided) is sent alongside `password_confirmation` per OneLogin's schema.

### `async update_user(user_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PUT /api/2/users/{user_id}` — patches arbitrary fields.

### `async delete_user(user_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /api/2/users/{user_id}`.

### `async search_users(query, limit=50) -> Dict`
GET `/api/2/users` with `?email=` when `query` contains `@`, otherwise `?username=`.

### `async set_user_state(user_id, state) -> Dict`
`PUT /api/2/users/{user_id}/state` — `state` must be `0` (suspend) or `1` (activate); any other value raises `ValueError`.

### `async assign_role_to_user(user_id, role_ids) -> Dict`
`POST /api/2/users/{user_id}/add_roles` — body: `{"role_id_array": role_ids}`.

### `async list_user_apps(user_id) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/users/{user_id}/apps`.

### `async list_user_roles(user_id) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/users/{user_id}/roles`.

### `async set_user_roles(user_id, role_ids) -> Dict`
`PUT /api/2/users/{user_id}/roles` — replaces the user's role set.

children.title: Apps, Roles, Groups
children.content:
### `async list_apps(limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/apps`.

### `async get_app(app_id) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/apps/{app_id}`.

### `async assign_app_to_user(user_id, app_id) -> Dict`
`POST /api/2/users/{user_id}/apps` — body: `{"app_id": app_id}`.

### `async list_roles(limit=50, after_cursor=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/roles` with cursor pagination.

### `async get_role(role_id) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/roles/{role_id}`.

### `async list_groups(limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/groups`.

### `async get_group(group_id) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/groups/{group_id}`.

children.title: Privileges, Mappings, Events
children.content:
### `async list_privileges() -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/privileges` — custom privileges defined in the tenant.

### `async list_mappings() -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/mappings` — user-to-app mapping rules.

### `async list_events(limit=50, since=None, event_type_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/events` — audit log, filterable by `since` (RFC 3339 timestamp) and `event_type_id`.

### `async get_event(event_id) -> Dict`
`GET /api/2/events/{event_id}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
OneLoginError                            # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── OneLoginAuthError                    # 401 / 403
├── OneLoginBadRequestError              # 400
├── OneLoginNotFoundError                # 404
├── OneLoginConflictError                # 409
├── OneLoginRateLimitError               # 429 — retry_after_s
└── OneLoginServerError                  # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
OneLoginNetworkError = OneLoginServerError
OneLoginNotFound     = OneLoginNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 401 | Clear cached token → re-`authenticate()` → replay original request once. If still 401 → raise `OneLoginAuthError` |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After`; retry once with backoff |
| 5xx | Retry once with backoff (1 s base, exponential); raise `OneLoginServerError` if still failing |
| `httpx.RequestError` | Wrap in `OneLoginNetworkError` |

### Connector-level retry (`helpers/utils.py::with_retry`)

The public methods wrap the http client in `with_retry(max_retries=3)` to absorb flaky transport above and beyond the HTTP-client's per-call retry. Backoff factor 2, max delay 32 s, jitter ±0.5 s.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)         — logged as warning
Network errors → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, AUTHENTICATED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, AUTHENTICATED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | One of `subdomain`, `client_id`, `client_secret` blank | Re-enter all three in ACP. The `message` field lists which keys are missing. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from OneLogin — credential's permission level lacks read scope | In OneLogin → Developers → API Credentials, edit the credential and grant `Read all` (or `Manage all` for write surfaces). |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` after a long idle | Cached token expired AND refresh fails | Verify the credential has not been deleted in OneLogin. Rotate `client_secret` if needed and update the install field. |
| `onelogin.request.retry` warnings spike with status=429 | Hitting OneLogin's per-tenant rate limit | The client retries with backoff using `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` (it's a client-side soft cap) or stagger batched calls. |
| 404 on `get_user(...)` | Wrong user ID (OneLogin IDs are numeric, not email) | Use `search_users(email=...)` first to resolve the numeric ID. |
| `ValueError: state must be 0 (suspend) or 1 (activate)` on `set_user_state` | Caller passed `2` or another value | OneLogin's `/users/{id}/state` only accepts 0 or 1. Use `update_user(...)` for finer-grained status changes (e.g. `status_locked`). |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'OneLoginNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without the back-compat alias | Already fixed — `OneLoginNetworkError = OneLoginServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| Authorization header on token endpoint missing | Custom HTTP client bypassed the connector | The connector signs the token endpoint with HTTP Basic; never POST `client_id` + `client_secret` in the body — OneLogin v2 expects Basic auth. |
